title: 10 best herbal teas for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jet lag are a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms of time zone changes. These teas contain a blend of herbs that help regulate the body's internal clock, reduce fatigue, and promote relaxation. Examples of herbal teas that can help with jet lag include peppermint, lavender, and valerian root tea. Drinking these teas before bedtime can improve sleep quality, allowing travelers to feel more rested and refreshed upon arrival. This can greatly impact daily life, as it enables people to be more productive, focused, and energized during their trip or after returning home.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels. The active compounds in valerian, such as valeric acid and isovaleric acid, interact with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle to regulate melatonin production. This helps to realign the body's internal clock to the new time zone, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep during local bedtime hours. Additionally, valerian teas have a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing fatigue and promoting a restful night's sleep after a long-haul flight.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian tea's sedative and calming properties can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and restlessness caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and calmness.

GABA-boosting properties: Valerian tea contains compounds that increase the production of GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles, making it easier to fall asleep after crossing time zones.

Adaptogenic properties: Valerian tea's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to changes in its normal rhythms and habits, reducing the impact of jet lag on the body's natural circadian rhythms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used to make teas due to their rich concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and ease symptoms of jet lag.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the valerian plant, which contains a higher concentration of essential oils than the leaves or stems, making it ideal for tea production.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour over the herb in the cup or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea strainer.
Enjoy the tea within 30 minutes of waking up, ideally with a light meal or snack.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with jet lag because it contains a blend of calming and invigorating herbs that can help regulate the body's internal clock. The caffeine in tea, although less than coffee, can provide a gentle boost to wakefulness without causing jitters or an energy crash later on. Meanwhile, herbs like chamomile and lavender can soothe the mind and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep at your destination time.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal teas like ashwagandha, ginseng, and rhodiola rosea contain adaptogenic compounds that help your body adjust to changes in environment and routine, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Antioxidants: Herbal teas like green tea, peppermint, and lemon balm are rich in antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by traveling across time zones, promoting overall well-being and recovery.

Aromatics and calming compounds: Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root contain aromatics and calming compounds that can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and restlessness, and regulate sleep patterns disrupted by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their adaptogenic properties, tea roots help regulate body functions and promote a sense of balance and calm.
Rhyzomes: With their rich content of caffeine and other alkaloids, tea rhyzomes can help stimulate circulation, improve mood, and increase alertness.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried camellia sinensis leaves and combine them with equal parts of lemon balm and passionflower in a tea infuser.
Heat filtered water to 200-212 degrees fahrenheit and steep the herb mixture for 7-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add one teaspoon of honey to help reduce stress and anxiety.
Drink the tea immediately after waking up on your destination day to help regulate your circadian rhythms.
Repeat this process every 2 hours throughout the day as needed to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and insomnia.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with jet lag because it contains a calming compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress. The tea's gentle soothing effects can help alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, anxiety, and insomnia. Additionally, lemon balm's natural sedative properties can promote relaxation and ease the transition to a new time zone, making it an excellent remedy for travelers to incorporate into their routine before and during long-distance trips.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Lemon balm tea's adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to new sleep schedules, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

Sedative Effects: The sedative compounds in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, promote relaxation, calmness, and drowsiness, which can help individuals overcome the effects of jet lag-related insomnia.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, which can contribute to fatigue, irritability, and other symptoms associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, lemon balm roots have natural adaptogenic properties that can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to promote relaxation, calmness, and reduced anxiety – all common complaints when traveling across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and measure out two teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb in hot water for 7-10 minutes allowing the bitter flavor to mellow slightly.
Strain the liquid carefully into a teapot or mug discarding the solids completely.
Add one teaspoon of honey or natural sweetener if desired and stir well to combine evenly.
Enjoy the warm soothing tea within 30 minutes of waking up for optimal jet lag relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms. The tea contains a compound called passifloric acid, which has been shown to synchronize the body's internal clock with the external environment. This allows the body to adjust to new time zones more quickly and efficiently, reducing the effects of fatigue, grogginess, and disorientation that often come with jet lag.
remedy_properties: Passionflower flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, helping to ease the stress and discomfort associated with jet lag.
Vitamin C: Maypop tea is a rich source of vitamin C, which can help boost the immune system and combat fatigue, common symptoms of jet lag.
Hesperidin: This flavonoid has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate headaches and other physical symptoms that can occur when traveling across time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their adaptogenic properties, Maypop roots help regulate body clock rhythms and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Fruits: The fruits of the Maypop plant contain a natural compound that helps adjust the body's circadian rhythm, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried passiflora incarnata flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes in a large tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve, if desired, for added sweetness.
Pour the tea into a thermos or glass and consume immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with jet lag because its soothing properties can calm the mind and body, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone. The tea's calming effects can also help reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, making it easier to adjust to a new environment. Additionally, chamomile's natural sedative properties can aid in establishing a consistent sleep pattern, which is essential for combating jet lag. By promoting relaxation and regulating sleep, chamomile teas can help travelers recover from their journey more quickly.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile tea helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with jet lag, allowing for better sleep and relaxation.

Sedative effects: The apigenin and luteolin compounds in chamomile tea have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of insomnia and restlessness caused by travel across time zones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce fatigue and discomfort caused by jet lag, as well as alleviate any underlying inflammation or stress that may exacerbate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety caused by jet lag.
Flowers: Known for their calming effects and ability to soothe digestive issues often experienced during travel.
remedy_recipe: Gently warm one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers in a heat-resistant cup using boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or according to package instructions for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey as needed to sweeten and enhance flavor without altering effects.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking while still warm for maximum relaxation benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with jet lag because it contains compounds that can help regulate body rhythms. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to adjust to an individual's unique bodily needs, including those affected by travel across time zones. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress, licorice tea can ease the transition into a new sleep-wake cycle, helping to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive discomfort. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for long-distance travelers or those who frequently cross time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root's adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to stress and fatigue caused by travel, promoting resilience and reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue.
Glycyrrhizin content: The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag like headaches, muscle aches, and digestive issues.

Aldosterone regulation: Licorice root's ability to regulate aldosterone levels can help regulate sleep-wake cycles by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and stress, making them an ideal part for calming symptoms associated with jet lag.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate fatigue and travel-related discomfort when consumed as a tea.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of fresh water and pour it over the glycyrrhiza glabra powder in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired tea strength.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea immediately, taking small sips every hour as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap teas helps with jet lag because it contains compounds that promote relaxation, reduce stress, and regulate sleep patterns. The tea's calming effects help to quiet a racing mind, making it easier to fall asleep at your destination time zone. Additionally, skullcap's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to new environments, reducing the shock of travel-induced fatigue and promoting a smooth transition back into your daily routine.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Skullcap contains adaptogenic compounds like baicalin, which helps to regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms and reduce stress, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.

Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in skullcap tea may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting overall well-being and reducing fatigue.

GABA-boosting effects: Skullcap has been shown to increase the levels of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) in the brain, which can help to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and improve sleep quality, all of which are common symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain sesquiterpenoid lactones, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety-related sleep disturbances.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, they help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried scutellaria baicalensis root powder.
Heat two cups of water in a pot until it reaches a boiling point.
Add the powdered root to the boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool for five minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date teas helps with jet lag because its unique blend of herbs, including Jujube dates, Ginseng, and Astragalus, works to regulate the body's internal clock and promote a sense of calm. The tea's calming properties help alleviate stress and anxiety caused by travel, allowing the body to naturally adjust to new time zones. Additionally, the tea's adaptogenic qualities help support the body's natural rhythms, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite unfamiliar environments.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Chinese Date Tea helps the body adapt to changes in environment, stress, and sleep patterns, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Digestive benefits: The tea's soothing effects on the digestive system can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and stomach upset often associated with jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to local cuisine and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Chinese Date Tea's rich antioxidant content helps combat oxidative stress caused by long-haul flights, while its anti-inflammatory properties may reduce fatigue, muscle soreness, and joint pain commonly experienced during and after travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue.
Rhyzomes: Containing coumarins and lignans, these underground stems have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate jet lag-induced stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried jujube fruit in 30g quantities for each serving.
Combine the dried fruit with 200ml boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl or teapot.
Add honey to taste as a sweetener if desired and stir well before serving.
Consume the warm tea immediately or let it cool to room temperature within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cucumber teas helps with jet lag because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the body's natural rhythms. The refreshing properties of cucumber help to revitalize and rehydrate the skin, while its cooling effects ease any discomfort or fatigue caused by traveling across time zones. Additionally, the tea's calming nature promotes a restful sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new environment and minimize the disruption caused by jet lag.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Cucumber tea contains adaptogens like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help your body adjust to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and fatigue associated with jet lag.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cucumber's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as fisetin, can help reduce inflammation caused by changes in altitude, temperature, and humidity, making it easier to adjust to new surroundings.

Hydrating effects: The high water content of cucumber tea helps replenish lost fluids and electrolytes during travel, promoting hydration and reducing the severity of jet lag symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cucumber roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and stress.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, contains antioxidants that promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, making it a natural remedy for jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cucumis sativus leaves and flowers, measuring 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the harvested ingredients with 8 ounces of boiling water, steeping for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant cup, discarding solids immediately.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired, before drinking the warm tea within 15-30 minutes of waking up.
Repeat the process every 6-8 hours for a total of 2-3 doses per day for optimal results and jet lag relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild rose teas helps with jet lag because of its unique combination of soothing and adaptogenic properties. The calming effects of wild rose help to regulate the body's natural rhythms, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. The herbal tea also contains adaptogens that help the body adjust to new environments, promoting a sense of balance and normalcy. By sipping on warm cups of wild rose tea, travelers can ease the symptoms of jet lag and get a good night's sleep, no matter where their journey takes them.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Wild rose tea helps your body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag. This allows you to better adjust to new time zones and schedules.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants in wild rose tea help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel and time changes, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag like headaches and insomnia.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: Wild rose tea's gentle, anti-inflammatory effects on the digestive system can help alleviate nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea that often accompany jet lag, allowing you to feel more comfortable and relaxed during travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rose roots are a natural sedative, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce insomnia caused by jet lag.
Barks: Wild rose bark contains salicin, similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate headaches and body aches often experienced during jet lag recovery.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried rosa laxa flowers into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a kettle and let it cool for five minutes.
Pour the cooled water over the dried rosa laxa flowers in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes before straining the tea leaves.
Drink the tea immediately after straining, taking three cups throughout the day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal teas is to consume calming blends containing herbs like valerian root, lavender, and chamomile. These herbs promote relaxation and help regulate sleep patterns. Try drinking a warm cup of tea 30 minutes before bedtime to calm your mind and body. Additionally, peppermint and ginger teas can aid digestion and reduce nausea, common symptoms of jet lag.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal teas are fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness. Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root promote relaxation and help regulate sleep patterns. Peppermint tea can alleviate stress and anxiety, while ginger tea soothes digestive issues often associated with travel. These teas offer a natural and calming solution to alleviate symptoms similar to jet lag without the side effects of medication.
